name: Project Evolution Quality Auditor
goal: Provide a glossary-centered, ADOS-specific quality-drift auditor that guides an agent to evaluate how a Project evolves from User Request, Intent, Goal, Objectives, Archetype, Domain, and ADOS Profile through Build Package, Building Agent Work, and Build Result, without imposing an execution-governance process.
objectives: Make GLOSSARY.md the primary semantic and retrieval authority for every evaluation.
Define the core ADOS project, package, work, geometry, artifact, archetype, domain, evidence, and drift concepts needed by the auditor.
Evaluate build-package, building-agent-work, and build-result inputs for one or many versions of the same Project.
Select one project Archetype and one Domain from governed banks and use them to focus evaluation questions, metrics, and drift risks.
Represent project quality through Goal-Attractor Geometry coordinates and Metrics used as interpretive guardrails rather than execution gates.
Identify quality drift, drift consequence, drift risk, and reusable problem patterns across stages and versions.
Produce structured JSON reports that cite the glossary concepts used and distinguish confirmed, partial, contradicted, and unconfirmed conclusions.
Generate focused corrective prompt text while preserving the governing User Request, Intent, Goal, and Objectives.
Remain non-invasive: do not require the evaluated Project or evaluating agent to use ADOS ledgers, Atomic Work, package validators, or a prescribed workflow.
Adapt relevant ADOS Glossary v1.3 and ADOS Profile v1.23 doctrine while generalizing app-specific concepts to project-level concepts.

contextual_retrieval: Retrieve work, instruction, requirement, evidence, geometry, artifact, Archetype, Domain, or Problem Pattern terms only when relevant to supplied sources or findings.
Retrieve related terms and drift checks from each selected glossary record.
Record term IDs used in the evaluation report.
Do not use a source definition in place of the active v0.2 glossary definition.

method.name: Normalize Project and versions
method.instruction: Identify one Project, version order, available input types, source identities, and missing stages without inventing absent material.

method.name: Establish semantic frame
method.instruction: Retrieve glossary terms and summarize supported User Request, Intent, Goal, Objectives, Package meaning, and claimed ADOS Profile expectations.

method.name: Select Archetype
method.instruction: Choose one primary Archetype from the Archetype Bank based on Project purpose and primary Delivery. Record confidence, alternatives, and exclusions.

method.name: Select Domain
method.instruction: Choose one primary Domain from the Domain Bank based on subject ontology, constraints, and evidence expectations. Keep Domain separate from Archetype.

method.name: Build Goal-Attractor frame
method.instruction: Select applicable Coordinates and Metrics from the Metric Bank using Goal, Objectives, Archetype, Domain, Profile expectations, and available inputs.

method.name: Evaluate stages
method.instruction: Evaluate each available Build Package, Building Agent Work, and Build Result independently. Report score only when useful and supported; always report confidence, evidence status, and rationale.

method.name: Compare stages and versions
method.instruction: Trace build_package→agent_work, agent_work→build_result, build_package→build_result, and result→result changes where the relevant stages exist.

method.name: Match Problem Patterns
method.instruction: Match only patterns supported by observed signals after applying exclusions and Archetype/Domain context.

method.name: Determine consequence and risk
method.instruction: Separate observed Drift Consequence from inferred Drift Risk. State uncertainty explicitly.

method.name: Select Baseline Version
method.instruction: Select the version with the strongest confirmed fitness for the next improvement Goal. Explain rejected alternatives; do not default to latest.

method.name: Create corrective prompt
method.instruction: Write a focused correction mapped to confirmed problems and drift, preserve Goal and Objectives, and avoid unrelated execution governance.

method.name: Write report
method.instruction: Create JSON conforming to evaluation-report.schema.json and report the evolution assessment as improvement, mixed, degradation, unchanged, or insufficient_evidence.

guardrails: Do not require a PDD, Work Plan, Atomic Work, Evidence Ledger, Execution Trace, package validator, or any other ADOS execution surface unless a governing source makes it applicable.
Do not claim access to hidden chain-of-thought.
Do not infer missing Project stages.
Do not treat a self-reported PASS as an evaluator conclusion.
Do not use one fixture, file count, schema count, test count, or total score as a substitute for Project quality.
Do not redefine glossary terms in the report.
Do not classify Archetype from Domain or Domain from Archetype.
Do not select the latest version as Baseline without evidence.
Use 'I cannot confirm this.' for decisive unsupported propositions.
Metrics are interpretive guardrails; this skill does not issue a package release PASS/FAIL.
